speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: Here they’ll be, man. Some o’ their

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: plants are ill-rooted already. The least wind i’ th’

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: world will blow them down.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: Lepidus is high-colored.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: They have made him drink alms-drink.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: As they pinch one another by the

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: disposition, he cries out “No more,” reconciles

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: them to his entreaty and himself to th’ drink.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: But it raises the greater war between

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: him and his discretion.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: Why, this it is to have a name in great

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: men’s fellowship. I had as lief have a reed that will

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: do me no service as a partisan I could not heave.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: To be called into a huge sphere, and not

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: to be seen to move in ’t, are the holes where eyes

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: should be, which pitifully disaster the cheeks.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thus do they, sir: they take the flow o’ th’ Nile

speaker: ANTONY
text: By certain scales i’ th’ Pyramid; they know

speaker: ANTONY
text: By th’ height, the lowness, or the mean if dearth

speaker: ANTONY
text: Or foison follow. The higher Nilus swells,

speaker: ANTONY
text: The more it promises. As it ebbs, the seedsman

speaker: ANTONY
text: Upon the slime and ooze scatters his grain,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And shortly comes to harvest.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: You’ve strange serpents there?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Ay, Lepidus.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: Your serpent of Egypt is bred now of your

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: mud by the operation of your sun; so is your

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: crocodile.

speaker: ANTONY
text: They are so.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Sit, and some wine. A health to Lepidus!

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: I am not so well as I should be, but I’ll ne’er

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: out.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Not till you have slept. I fear me

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: you’ll be in till then.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: Nay, certainly, I have heard the Ptolemies’

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: pyramises are very goodly things. Without contradiction

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: I have heard that.

speaker: MENAS
text: Pompey, a word.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Say in mine ear what is ’t.

speaker: MENAS
text: Forsake thy seat, I do beseech thee, captain,

speaker: MENAS
text: And hear me speak a word.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Forbear me till anon.—This wine for Lepidus!

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: What manner o’ thing is your crocodile?

speaker: ANTONY
text: It is shaped, sir, like itself, and it is as broad as

speaker: ANTONY
text: it hath breadth. It is just so high as it is, and moves

speaker: ANTONY
text: with it own organs. It lives by that which nourisheth

speaker: ANTONY
text: it, and the elements once out of it, it

speaker: ANTONY
text: transmigrates.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: What color is it of?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of it own color too.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: ’Tis a strange serpent.

speaker: ANTONY
text: ’Tis so, and the tears of it are wet.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Will this description satisfy

speaker: CAESAR
text: him?

speaker: ANTONY
text: With the health that Pompey gives him, else he

speaker: ANTONY
text: is a very epicure.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Go hang, sir, hang! Tell me of that? Away!

speaker: POMPEY
text: Do as I bid you.—Where’s this cup I called for?

speaker: MENAS
text: If for the sake of merit thou wilt hear me,

speaker: MENAS
text: Rise from thy stool.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I think th’ art mad!

speaker: POMPEY
text: The matter?

speaker: MENAS
text: I have ever held my cap off to thy fortunes.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Thou hast served me with much faith. What’s else to say?—

speaker: POMPEY
text: Be jolly, lords.

speaker: ANTONY
text: These quicksands, Lepidus,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Keep off them, for you sink.

speaker: MENAS
text: Wilt thou be lord of all the world?

speaker: POMPEY
text: What sayst thou?

speaker: MENAS
text: Wilt thou be lord of the whole world? That’s twice.

speaker: POMPEY
text: How should that be?

speaker: MENAS
text: But entertain it,

speaker: MENAS
text: And though thou think me poor, I am the man

speaker: MENAS
text: Will give thee all the world.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Hast thou drunk well?

speaker: MENAS
text: No, Pompey, I have kept me from the cup.

speaker: MENAS
text: Thou art, if thou dar’st be, the earthly Jove.

speaker: MENAS
text: Whate’er the ocean pales or sky inclips

speaker: MENAS
text: Is thine, if thou wilt ha ’t.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Show me which way.

speaker: MENAS
text: These three world-sharers, these competitors,

speaker: MENAS
text: Are in thy vessel. Let me cut the cable,

speaker: MENAS
text: And when we are put off, fall to their throats.

speaker: MENAS
text: All there is thine.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Ah, this thou shouldst have done

speaker: POMPEY
text: And not have spoke on ’t! In me ’tis villainy;

speaker: POMPEY
text: In thee ’t had been good service. Thou must know

speaker: POMPEY
text: ’Tis not my profit that does lead mine honor;

speaker: POMPEY
text: Mine honor, it. Repent that e’er thy tongue

speaker: POMPEY
text: Hath so betrayed thine act. Being done unknown,

speaker: POMPEY
text: I should have found it afterwards well done,

speaker: POMPEY
text: But must condemn it now. Desist and drink.

speaker: MENAS
text: For this

speaker: MENAS
text: I’ll never follow thy palled fortunes more.

speaker: MENAS
text: Who seeks and will not take when once ’tis offered

speaker: MENAS
text: Shall never find it more.

speaker: POMPEY
text: This health to Lepidus!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Bear him ashore.—I’ll pledge it for him, Pompey.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Here’s to thee, Menas.

speaker: MENAS
text: Enobarbus, welcome.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Fill till the cup be hid.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: There’s a strong fellow, Menas.

speaker: MENAS
text: Why?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: He bears

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The third part of the world, man. Seest not?

speaker: MENAS
text: The third part, then, is drunk. Would it were all,

speaker: MENAS
text: That it might go on wheels.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Drink thou. Increase the reels.

speaker: MENAS
text: Come.

speaker: POMPEY
text: This is not yet an Alexandrian feast.

speaker: ANTONY
text: It ripens towards it. Strike the vessels, ho!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Here’s to Caesar.

speaker: CAESAR
text: I could well forbear ’t.

speaker: CAESAR
text: It’s monstrous labor when I wash my brain

speaker: CAESAR
text: And it grows fouler.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Be a child o’ th’ time.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Possess it, I’ll make answer.

speaker: CAESAR
text: But I had rather fast from all, four days,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Than drink so much in one.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Ha, my brave emperor,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Shall we dance now the Egyptian bacchanals

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: And celebrate our drink?

speaker: POMPEY
text: Let’s ha ’t, good soldier.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Come, let’s all take hands

speaker: ANTONY
text: Till that the conquering wine hath steeped our sense

speaker: ANTONY
text: In soft and delicate Lethe.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: All take hands.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Make battery to our ears with the loud music,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The while I’ll place you; then the boy shall sing.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The holding every man shall beat as loud

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: As his strong sides can volley.

speaker: BOY
text: Come, thou monarch of the vine,

speaker: BOY
text: Plumpy Bacchus, with pink eyne.

speaker: BOY
text: In thy vats our cares be drowned.

speaker: BOY
text: With thy grapes our hairs be crowned.

speaker: ALL
text: Cup us till the world go round,

speaker: ALL
text: Cup us till the world go round.

speaker: CAESAR
text: What would you more?—Pompey, goodnight.— Good brother,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Let me request you off. Our graver business

speaker: CAESAR
text: Frowns at this levity.—Gentle lords, let’s part.

speaker: CAESAR
text: You see we have burnt our cheeks. Strong Enobarb

speaker: CAESAR
text: Is weaker than the wine, and mine own tongue

speaker: CAESAR
text: Splits what it speaks. The wild disguise hath almost

speaker: CAESAR
text: Anticked us all. What needs more words? Goodnight.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Good Antony, your hand.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I’ll try you on the shore.

speaker: ANTONY
text: And shall, sir. Give ’s your hand.

speaker: POMPEY
text: O, Antony, you have my father’s house.

speaker: POMPEY
text: But what? We are friends! Come down into the boat.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Take heed you fall not.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Menas, I’ll not on shore.

speaker: MENAS
text: No, to my cabin. These drums, these trumpets, flutes! What!

speaker: MENAS
text: Let Neptune hear we bid a loud farewell

speaker: MENAS
text: To these great fellows. Sound and be hanged. Sound out!

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Hoo, says ’a! There’s my cap!

speaker: MENAS
text: Hoo! Noble captain, come.
